# tests/fixtures — guest-written test images

## minix-v1-2048.img (Phase 16 M1)

A 2 MB MINIX v1 filesystem **written by ELKS itself** — the guest's
own `mkfs`, shell redirects, `cp`, and `mv`, run inside the emulator.
The point: `src/disk/minix-fs.ts` is tested against the REAL writer,
not against anyone's reading of the spec.

**Provenance** (2026-07-15): generated by
`tests/integration/minix-fixture-gen.test.ts` (env-gated; skipped in
CI). It boots `reference/elks-images-hd/hd32-minix.img` headlessly
with a blank secondary on the canonical 8086 KB geometry (311×4×13),
runs `mkfs /dev/hdb 2048` in the guest, populates the tree below,
`sync` + `umount`, verifies with the guest's `fsck /dev/hdb` (exit 0,
silent = clean), snapshots via `snapshot-secondary`, and commits the
first 2048 KB. Regenerate with:

```
MINIX_FIXTURE_GEN=1 npx vitest run tests/integration/minix-fixture-gen.test.ts
```

…and keep `tests/unit/minix-fs.test.ts`'s expected tree in lockstep.

**The tree** (all content derivable host-side; sizes from the guest's
own `ls -l`):

| path | bytes | why it exists |
|---|---|---|
| `/README.txt` | 29 | small direct-zone file |
| `/abcdefghijklmn` | 9 | exactly-14-char name — the v1 name limit |
| `/seed.txt` | 976 | the 61-byte line ×16 the big files build from |
| `/big.txt` | 15,744 | crosses the 7-KB **indirect** boundary; 16 distinct `chunk-<x>` sections so indirect-index bugs can't hide behind repeating blocks |
| `/huge.txt` | 999,424 | crosses the 519-KB **double-indirect** boundary; the 61-byte line is coprime with the 1 KB block, so every block carries a distinct phase |
| `/binblob` | 10,176 | `cp /bin/ls` — a real ELKS a.out executable (binary content, NUL bytes) |
| `/dir1/nested.txt` | 20 | file in a subdirectory |
| `/dir1/sub/empty.txt` | 0 | zero-byte file |
| *(deleted)* `/doomed.txt` | — | created then `rm`'d, leaving an inode-0 directory slot for the skip-deleted path |

Superblock facts the unit tests pin: magic `0x137F`, 14-char names,
`s_nzones` 2048, `s_log_zone_size` 0.
